page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 480 GB NVMe SSD. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and does not contain any GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU test and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests all rank in the top tier (top 10%). Conversely, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average tier (middle 50%). Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, also register average performance. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with a small 135M model, but exhibits weak prompt processing performance with a medium 7B model.

The primary tradeoff of this server configuration lies in its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads despite the high-performing AMD EPYC-Turin architecture. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU alongside fast NVMe storage, making it a viable option for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded performance without requiring dedicated CPU cores. Suitable workloads include general-purpose web serving, database operations, redis caching, and lightweight text processing or small-scale model inference.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU allocation presents potential performance variability under sustained multi-core workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmarks. Multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently perform in the average tier. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated NVMe storage, this instance provides qualitative resource density for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and lightweight single-threaded applications.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is a virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for single-threaded workloads and general-purpose applications.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
480 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Suitable for web serving, Redis, and database workloads
